title: Research and Development in the Social Sciences and Humanities

narrative.executive_view: This is a heterogeneous, low-confidence Conditional screen rather than a roll-up thesis. AI can materially compress research preparation, coding, analysis and reporting, but much of the likely dominant sponsored-research book is effort-priced or cost-reimbursable, so the client captures a substantial share of efficiency. The code also mixes program evaluation and survey work with cultural-resource management (CRM) and behavioral programs; those businesses have different demand, buyers and economics [S1][S3][S4].
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AAPOR reports that AI is being used across questionnaire design, interviewing, data processing, analysis and reporting, while also distinguishing synthetic outputs from human research participants [S2]. That maps to substantial assistance for literature review, survey drafting, coding, statistical work, documentation and clerical operations. It does not replace study design accountability, sponsor interpretation, physical fieldwork, human-delivered behavioral services or professional responsibility for archaeological work.
narrative.value_capture: Federal cost-reimbursement contracts pay allowable incurred costs up to an estimated-cost ceiling, so labor-hour reduction commonly reduces the cost base rather than creating a like-for-like supplier margin windfall [S1]. Fixed-price CRM projects can retain more of the saving, but the code-level revenue mix is not observed. Contract vehicles, past performance and qualified staff create some relationship protection, while sponsors can use AI internally for generic analysis; this is why capture is deliberately low rather than treating statutory fieldwork as a capture moat.
narrative.adoption_timing: AAPOR's 2026 responsible-AI report says adoption is accelerating and identifies use across the survey-research lifecycle [S2]. It does not publish a representative adoption percentage for US lower-middle-market NAICS 541720 firms, so the 25% current-use selection and three-year t50 are explicit estimates rather than reported statistics. The central case assumes task-level tooling spreads before firms materially redesign delivery and pricing.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic dataset identifies 258 firms in the estimated $1-10M EBITDA band, but it does not tell us whether they are for-profit, founder-dependent, nonprofit-affiliated, or exposed to the same end markets. ACRA confirms that CRM firms are a real, firm-based subsegment, but it does not establish an owner-age or buyer-count statistic for this NAICS code [S5]. Entry and exit multiples are therefore conservative, unsupported-by-a-code-specific transaction database estimates and should not be used to underwrite a deal.
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal value is PRESSURED. National Park Service standards require qualified archaeological professionals with education and field experience for archaeological activities, preserving a physical and credentialed component of CRM demand [S4]. Conversely, survey/evaluation analysis is exposed to AI-enabled substitution and to volatile federal demand: the Department of Education OIG reports 129 contract terminations totaling $1.3 billion in early 2025, including educational-study contracts [S3]. Neither mechanism proves the revenue-weighted mix, so the terminal classification is cautious.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The principal risk is misclassification: NAICS 541720 combines federal and foundation research contractors, CRM archaeology and behavioral-program organizations, while market research is elsewhere. The occupational mix is a five-digit proxy and may include roles not representative of a target firm. There is no fresh code-specific fee-model, owner-age, consolidator, or EBITDA-multiple dataset in this packet; all such selections are plainly low-confidence estimates with wide ranges. A target-level diligence pass should segment revenue, contract type, sponsor concentration, owner dependence and field-labor exposure before treating the screen as actionable.

sources.title: FAR Subpart 16.3 — Cost-Reimbursement Contracts
sources.what_it_supports: Federal cost-reimbursement contracts pay allowable incurred costs and are used where requirements or costs cannot be estimated accurately enough for fixed price.

sources.title: AAPOR Releases New Report from Task Force on Responsible AI Integration in Survey Research
sources.what_it_supports: AI is being used across questionnaire design, interviewing, data processing, analysis and reporting in survey research, with adoption accelerating.

sources.title: Review of U.S. Department of Education Changes in Staffing and Operations
sources.what_it_supports: Department of Education actions in early 2025 included 129 terminated contracts totaling $1.3 billion and terminated contracts related to educational studies.

sources.title: National Park Service Professional Qualifications Standards
sources.what_it_supports: Archaeological professional qualifications require a graduate degree plus field and professional experience, supporting the physical and credentialed character of the CRM subsegment.

sources.title: American Cultural Resources Association Mission
sources.what_it_supports: ACRA is a national trade association for cultural-resource-management firms of different sizes, types and specialties.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI exposure is high for research preparation, survey/document production, coding and analysis, but low for statutory field activity, human services and sponsor accountability.
ai_replaceable_share.plausible_range: 0.22-0.38 of total wage bill after role weighting
caveats: AAPOR describes survey research broadly, not task shares inside US NAICS 541720 lower-middle-market firms.
The supplied occupation mix is five-digit 54172 and the large residual combines unobserved field, research, data and program roles.
Adoption speed, federal funding shocks, pricing and terminal demand are not used to inflate these task fractions.

role_judgments.rationale: Drafting, coding and analysis automate partially; study design and research judgment persist.
Internal coordination automates, while principal relationships and accountability persist.
Routine document production, scheduling and data entry are highly exposed.
Literature, coding and report work automate; methodological judgment remains.
Documentation is exposed but behavioral-service delivery is relational.
Scheduling and status work automate while sponsor coordination persists.
Reporting assists, but survey, excavation and field responsibility remain physical and qualified work.
Operational analysis and documentation are exposed, with client-specific judgment retained.
Scheduling, correspondence and records handling are highly exposed.
Notes and administration automate, but counseling is human-delivered.
The large unlisted tail mixes statisticians and data staff with field, IT and program roles; a moderate fraction avoids assuming all research labor is desk analysis.

pi_dist.rationale: Cost-reimbursement and effort-priced sponsored work pass much of a labor-hour saving to the client; fixed-price CRM can retain more but is not assumed dominant.
caveats: FAR describes an available federal contract type, not the NAICS 541720 revenue-weighted contract mix.
The fixed-price CRM share versus sponsored research share is not observed.

pi_moat.rationale: Qualified personnel, past performance and sponsor relationships create moderate resistance, but generic research analysis can be insourced or rebid with AI.
caveats: NPS qualifications and ACRA membership describe the CRM subsegment, not a code-wide switching-cost measurement.
Credentialed physical fieldwork supports terminal demand separately and is not counted as a second capture mechanism.

t50_years.rationale: AAPOR documents active AI integration across the lifecycle; a three-year crossing from an estimated 25% current effective adoption allows for procurement, validation and pricing redesign.
caveats: AAPOR does not report a representative current-adoption rate or diffusion curve for US lower-middle-market 541720 firms.
This is delivery adoption, not client enthusiasm or a count of experimental AI use.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: The selection represents material, repeatable workflow use below the 50% boundary, not the presence of isolated pilots.
caveats: No source in this packet directly measures current adoption for the target industry, geography and size band.

historical_analogs.value: Survey research previously absorbed CATI, web collection, coding software and statistical tools while preserving methodological oversight; generative AI extends automation into drafting, coding and analysis but faces validity constraints for human-subject inference.
historical_analogs.rationale: AAPOR's lifecycle framing and treatment of synthetic outputs support an incremental-workflow analogy rather than an assumption that all human research is replaced.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Incremental augmentation to substantial analytical substitution
caveats: The analogy is qualitative and does not independently set the selected t50 or role fractions.

selection.rationale: No fresh US 541720 owner-age statistic was found, so a conservative unmeasured estimate is used.
caveats: This is not evidence of a sector-wide succession wave.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No 541720-specific succession shortage was documented in the fresh source set.
caveats: Individual firms may still have material succession risk.

active_consolidators.rationale: CRM has an organized firm ecosystem, but no source here measures active PE or strategic bidders for the full code; two is a deliberately low proxy count.
caveats: ACRA membership is not evidence of a consolidator count or of acquisition demand.
Used only for buyability, not for AI exposure or adoption.

buy_mult.rationale: A conservative normalized entry multiple reflects small, heterogeneous, sponsor-dependent professional-service targets without asserting a sourced transaction median.
caveats: No code-specific, target-band EBITDA transaction source was found in the fresh pass.
Owner compensation, nonprofit contamination, contract concentration and seasonal field labor require target-level normalization.

exit_mult.rationale: A modest one-turn expansion is conditional on a buyer proving recurring sponsor relationships, disciplined delivery and less founder dependence; it is not a sourced exit-market forecast.
caveats: No code-specific exit multiple or observed multiple expansion supports this selection.
The assumed expansion may disappear if AI pressure or federal demand shocks reduce durable earnings.

pricing_structure.value: Mixed sponsored cost-reimbursement or effort-priced research and fixed-price CRM projects; the code-level revenue mix is not observed.
pricing_structure.rationale: FAR establishes the cost-reimbursement mechanism relevant to federal sponsored research; the selection explicitly leaves CRM fixed-price exposure uncertain.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Cost/effort-priced sponsored research to fixed-price CRM-heavy book
caveats: FAR is not evidence that a majority of target-firm revenue uses this contract type.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Role-level task exposure is set from work content, while adoption timing is a separate estimate based on AAPOR's lifecycle evidence. Federal demand shocks, ownership and deal activity do not increase V or A.

terminal_value.justification: Qualified physical CRM work and some sponsor-facing expertise survive, but AI threatens generic analysis and evaluation products and federal educational-study contracting has been volatile. The revenue-weighted dominant subsegment is not observed.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: All external evidence is a federal-contracting, survey-research, CRM-qualification, educational-contracting or trade-association proxy. None is presented as a direct lower-middle-market 541720 measurement.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Cost-reimbursement and effort-priced contracts can transmit labor savings to sponsors; that channel is reflected only in pi_dist. Physical/credentialed work is considered in terminal demand, not used again to raise capture.

dataset_mismatch.description: Labor share combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts, while the occupation mix is five-digit 54172 rather than six-digit 541720. The listed roles cover only about 40.5% of wage share, making the residual judgment material.
sensitivity: A more field- and program-heavy target workforce would lower task exposure.
A different current revenue base could alter labor-share and target-band economics.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Sponsored survey and program-evaluation research, selected as a hypothesis rather than measured fact.
market_boundary.selection_basis: The prompt's occupation and industry evidence indicates materially different subsegments; the central case weights the sponsored-research mechanism while explicitly retaining CRM and behavioral-program uncertainty.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A faster or slower shift from workflow experiments to material delivery adoption changes A directly.
The unobserved mix of cost/effort pricing versus fixed-price CRM changes whether AI savings accrue to the operator.
No target-band transaction data anchors the entry or exit estimates, so M can move sharply.
The large residual can be analyst-heavy or field/program-heavy, materially changing available labor savings.

reviewer_note: Do not use this screen to select a company. Underwrite individual targets by contract type, sponsor concentration, revenue subsegment, field-labor requirements, owner dependence and evidence of AI-enabled price compression.
